differential_diagnoses: Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia with metastatic infection (e.g., spondylodiscitis, sternoclavicular septic arthritis)
Acute aortic syndrome (penetrating aortic ulcer or dissection)
Takotsubo cardiomyopathy secondary to systemic infection
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) immediately to evaluate for vegetations and valvular involvement
Obtain emergent CTA of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with delayed venous phase to evaluate the aortic mural thickening and possible mycotic aneurysm
Continue vancomycin and cefazolin for MSSA bacteremia until source control is achieved
Consult Infectious Disease and Cardiothoracic Surgery regarding suspected vascular infection
Perform serial physical examinations focusing on new murmurs, vascular compromise, or point tenderness over the spine and chest wall
Monitor blood cultures to document clearance of S. aureus bacteremia

differential_diagnoses: Aortic mycotic aneurysm with contained rupture
Infective endocarditis with systemic septic emboli
next_steps: Immediate CTA of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with dedicated aortic protocol to evaluate for mycotic aneurysm expansion or rupture
Urgent transesophageal echocardiography to evaluate for valvular vegetations and aortic valve involvement
Consultation with Cardiothoracic and Vascular Surgery for potential source control of the aortic/vascular infection
MRI of the thoracic spine to rule out spondylodiscitis, given the spinal canal findings and localized pain
Continue aggressive intravenous antistaphylococcal therapy (vancomycin and cefazolin) with daily blood culture monitoring
Strict blood pressure control (e.g., intravenous beta-blocker/nitroglycerin) to reduce aortic wall stress
